page: Amazon Web Services r6in.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, allowing 2 threads per core. The dedicated CPU allocation operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz, supported by a 54 MB L3 cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1D cache. It features 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local instance storage, meaning all storage is network-attached via Amazon Elastic Block Store. The instance is network and EBS optimized, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the r6in.2xlarge in the average range for most compute and memory tasks. Single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests yield scores of 1891.99 and 7709.54, respectively. Memory read-only bandwidth reaches 413.7 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 56.36 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, while write bandwidth is measured at 41.63 GB/sec. The instance handles Redis SET operations at 7.98 million operations per second and static web serving at 11.98 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized LLM inference tasks show weaker relative performance, indicating limitations for highly parallelized decompression or complex AI inference workloads.

Given its memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, the r6in.2xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high memory density relative to compute resources without the overhead of local storage. It is well-suited for memory-intensive applications such as relational and non-relational databases, caching layers like Redis, in-memory analytics, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from network-optimized throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services r6in.2xlarge is a memory-optimized, network-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, delivering 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate solid average performance across standard compute, memory bandwidth, and database operations, such as Redis SET operations at 7.98 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and larger LLM inference tasks. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and database deployments that rely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized, network-enhanced virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-density DDR4 RAM for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 8 vCPUs and 64 GB DDR4 RAM
Network and EBS optimized with a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation
No local storage or GPU resources included
Delivers 7.98 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and in-memory analytics
